title: List of fanon elements in continuity
content:
{{Top|real}}
{{Citation}}
Occasionally, elements developed by fans make their way into the official [[canon]]. This usually involves popular theories held by fans that are eventually accepted by authors as truth, however are sometimes more specific as ideas or artwork by a fan appearing on web sites or forums.

Below is a list of fanon elements that have been included into EU literature and thus have become canon.

==Fan film==
===Stacy from ''Pink Five''===
[[Stacy]], the main character from the ''[[Pink Five]]'' series of [[fan film]]s, made an appearance in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s novel [[Allegiance (novel)|''Allegiance'']].<ref name="Pink Five">{{Blogspot|subdomain=rop5|url=2007/01/geekza-3-canon-1.html|name=The Pink Five Production Blog|text=Geekza 3, Canon +1}}</ref>

==Fan art==
===[[Shaala Doneeta]]===
Adopted by [[Joe Corroney]] for use in ''[[CIS Shadowfeed Year's Start Fete Day Edition]]''. Sha'ala's character was based on artwork commissioned by Bryan K. Borgman of a Force adept created for his ''Star Wars'' RPG home game in early [[2000]].

===Hsiao vessels===
Various vessels created by [[Ansel Hsiao]], including the [[Vigil-class corvette|''Vigil''-class corvette]], the [[Altor-class replenishment ship|''Altor''-class replenishment ship]], the [[Allegiance-class battlecruiser|''Allegiance''-class battlecruiser]], the [[Assertor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Assertor''-class Star Dreadnought]], the [[Procursator-class Star Destroyer|''Procursator''-class Star Destroyer]], the [[Bellator-class dreadnought/Legends|''Bellator''-class dreadnought]], and [[Naval Station Validusia/Legends|Naval Station ''Validusia'']], appeared in ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' and were later entered into the [[Holocron continuity database]].<ref name="Fractalsponge comments">{{WebCite|url=http://bbs.stardestroyer.net/viewtopic.php?f=46&t=124353&sid=e8501d9d9e2ccc9a1c18226a2628097f&start=1575|text=Thread: Something big – Page 64|work=[http://bbs.stardestroyer.net StarDestroyer.Net BBS]|archivedate=20210426190033}}</ref>

==Fan theories==
===[[Grievous/Legends|Grievous's]] mask===
Adopted by [[Abel G. Peña]] for use in ''[[The Story of General Grievous: Lord of War]]'', the notion that [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] might eventually have obtained Grievous's mask originated in a forum thread at the [[Jedi Council Forums|Jedi Council]]. User Charles Phipps, aka '''Charlemagne19''', posed the question of how Grievous would do tactically against Thrawn, in response to which forumers Mike Cooper and Kyle Hartshorn, aka '''Rogue_Follower''', posited that it would depend on whether he had seen [[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]] art; namely, Grievous's mask.

===[[Trade Federation of Planets|Trade Federation]] founding date===
Webmaster [[Michael Potts]] extrapolated a founding date of [[350 BBY/Legends|350 BBY]] for the [[Trade Federation of Planets|Trade Federation]] based on available information, and was pleasantly surprised to see this time made canonical in [[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']].

===[[Hand of Judgment]]===
During a conversation he had with [[Timothy Zahn]] at the [[2004]] [[Wikipedia:Stellarcon|Stellarcon]], [[Albin Johnson]] came up with the idea of rogue vigilante [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. Zahn later included the idea, as the [[Hand of Judgment]], in his novel [[Allegiance (novel)|''Allegiance'']].

===[[Tràkata/Legends|Tràkata]]===
This [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber form]] was a widespread fan concept that was eventually included in the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]''.

===[[Endor disaster|"Endor Holocaust"]] theory===
The "Endor Holocaust" theory was first put forward by [[Curtis Saxton]] on his ''[[Star Wars Technical Commentaries]]'' fansite. Saxton claims that it is scientifically inevitable for the disaster to happen. Some sources, such as ''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]'' and the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] "[[Apocalypse Endor]]," mention the theory as in-universe Imperial propaganda or myth.

===[[Endor Moon Hoax]]===
The concept that [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] was a rogue moon, formerly orbiting a destroyed [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]], originated in the [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Return of the Jedi'' novelization]] and was reproduced in [[Curtis Saxton]]'s ''[[Star Wars Technical Commentaries]]'' [http://www.theforce.net/swtc/orbs.html#endor article], which elaborated on the gas giant's absence. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' acknowledged (and debunked) the theory, mentioning an in-universe "hoax" which wrongly claimed that the planet was destroyed in a cataclysmic event.

===Cos Palpatine===
Some fans speculated that Palpatine's full name was Cos Palpatine, derived from an early version of the ''Star Wars'' script where his name was originally [[Cos Dashit]].

Although Palpatine himself was not granted this name, his father was granted the name "Cosinga Palpatine" in the novel ''Darth Plagueis'', which, when shortened, is "Cos Palpatine."

==Fan nicknames==
Unnamed entities usually acquire fan-made nicknames, and sometimes, these make their way into canon.

===Jedi Exile===
[[Meetra Surik|Jedi Exile]] was a fanon nickname for this nameless character (called just exile in the game dialogues), however this has been adopted in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'', before she was given the name Meetra Surik in ''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]''.

===Padawan Massacre of Taris===
[[John Jackson Miller]] identified [[Wookieepedia]] as the source of the conjectural name "[[Padawan Massacre of Taris]]," which appeared in [[Knights of the Old Republic 11|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 11]].<ref name="Faraway">{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor06.html|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #6}}</ref>

===[[DP20 frigate]]===
The common fan designation of this ship as the "DP-20" or "DP20" was finally canonized in the revised version of [[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'']].

===Force kick===
The term "[[Force kick]]" is a joking reference to a poorly choreographed fighting instance in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. This sparked off a minor fad within the Star Wars-oriented part of the YTMND community, in which the term might have originated.

Eventually the Force kick received a canonical mention in ''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'', albeit as a joke, and then was fully canonized in ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''.

===[[Sith Triumvirate]]===
The name "Sith Triumvirate" was first coined for the trio of [[Kreia|Darth Traya]], [[Darth Sion]] and [[Darth Nihilus]] by [[Wookieepedia]] until authors [[John Jackson Miller]], [[Rodney Thompson]], [[Sterling Hershey]], and [[Abel G. Peña]] made the name for the group official in ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''.

==Fan languages==
[[File:TwilekiLanguage-JediKnights6.png|thumb|right|150px|The Twi'leki of ''Jedi Knights'' 6 is based on a fan dictionary.]]
During the early days of [[Wookieepedia]] in [[2006]], a point in time where less oversight existed for the website's editors,<ref name="Marv Mando">{{Twitter|username=NumidianPrime|url=status/1387446293883142148|name=Marv|date=2021-04-28|quote=Some of these translations were rough and seem to have often not been based on much from official material but back in the early days of the site there wasn't the same strict oversight there is today and it went mostly unchallenged.|archivedate=20210428163922}}</ref> Wookieepedia editor [[User:Doran|Doran]] added their own fan translations onto several pages relating to [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalorian leaders]]<ref name="Doran's idea">{{WookCite|url=Talk:Mandalore_the_First?diff=prev&oldid=370139|text=Talk: Mandalore the First, Revision as of 00:48, 29 March 2006}}</ref> by roughly<ref name="Doran's translation">{{WookCite|url=Talk:Spar?diff=prev&oldid=370187|text=Talk: Spar, Revision as of 01:27, 29 March 2006}}</ref> translating their names to [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']].<ref name="Doran's idea" /> These fanon translations would remain on Wookieepedia for years and spread across the fandom as if they were canonized names, with [[Mandalore the Ultimate]], in particular, allegedly being known as "''Te Ani'la Mand'alor''" in ''Mando'a''.<ref name="Marv 15">{{Twitter|username=NumidianPrime|url=status/1387446744363962368|name=Marv|date=2021-04-28|quote=Because of that, for the last fifteen years Mandalore the Ultimate has had the Mando'a title "Te Ani'la Mand'alor" at the top of his page. The name has been all over the Mando fandom since then, and same goes for other Mando'a names that as far as I can tell, Doran made up.|archivedate=20210428164118}}</ref> While these fan-translated names were ultimately removed from Wookieepedia in [[2021]] for being fanon,<ref name="Marv Removed">{{Twitter|username=NumidianPrime|url=status/1387535139471831041|name=Marv|date=2021-04-28|quote=The name has finally been removed from his page after fifteen years, which is good because it was baseless and inaccurate, but also a little sad because while it was fanon, it also became a (small) piece of actual SW history.|archivedate=20210428223217}}</ref> the [[2017]] canon reference book ''[[Star Wars: The Visual Encyclopedia]]'' had used the ''Te Ani'la'' name to establish the [[Tree of Te Ani'la|trees of ''Te Ani'la'']] as in-universe plants.<ref name="Visual Enc">''[[Star Wars: The Visual Encyclopedia]]''</ref> As such, while Mandalore the Ultimate's ''Mando'a'' name is fanon, the "''Te Ani'la''" name itself remains in official ''Star Wars'' lore, albeit only for those trees.

In the [[2025]] comic [[Jedi Knights 6|''Jedi Knights'' 6]], [[Jedi Knight]] [[Aayla Secura]] speaks in a form of the [[Twi'leki]] language<ref name="TwilekiConfirmed">{{Bluesky|marcguggenheim.bsky.social|post/3lwg2qzjyjc22|[[Marc Guggenheim]]|date=2025-08-15|quote=Hey! Thank you for noticing!|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/nGSSh}} {{C|In response to: "the first time in canon we've seen written out twi'lek language, by the way! nice pull!"}}</ref> that has its roots in a fanon [[Twi'lek]] dictionary from the Infinite Empire fandom forum. Before its usage in ''Jedi Knights'' 6, although some words had their roots in Legends sources, most of the language was fanmade and fanon only.<ref name="TwilekLanguage">{{Twitter|username=NumidianPrime|url=status/1956184815897862306|name=NumidianPrime|date=2025-08-14|quote=Had a hunch and managed to source most of this funeral speech Aayla gives in the new Jedi Knights #6 to a fanon Twi'lek dictionary I found on a forum (https://infiniteempirerpg.proboards.com/thread/1190/ryl-basic-dictionary), with some of the words having old Legends grounding and most being fanmade.|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/f2pum}}</ref>

==Fan culture==
===The [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]]===
[[Timothy Zahn]], in his novel ''[[Survivor's Quest]]'', named the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] after the international fan group of the [[501st Legion (fan organization)|same name]]. The unit would go on to be featured as [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] personal stormtrooper legion in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']] and be the focus of the game ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'', as well as being prominently featured in the book ''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'', the television series ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'', and many other works.

===The [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]]===
For years, fans have been referring to the [[The galaxy/Legends|''Star Wars'' galaxy]] as the [[Galaxy Far, Far Away|GFFA]]; an acronym of ''Galaxy Far, Far Away''. When [[Del Rey]] set out to create a new galactic government near the end of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], they christened it the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] as an homage to the fanon term.

===BBY/ABY calendar system===
The [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]] system used in many sources to represent dates in the ''Star Wars'' timeline originated as an out-of-universe dating system and, after becoming popular, was retconned into an in-universe system used by the New Republic.

===The [[Spotts TradeChip Company]]===
This company mentioned in [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 50]] released a set of Jedi trading holograms that the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] denounced as "random and arbitrary;" among such 'facts' [[Coleman Trebor/Legends|Coleman Trebor]] said ''"They have Master Yoda's species listed as [[Lannik (species)/Legends|Lannik]], and his midi-chlorian count at 4 million. That's just ridiculous"''. This, combined with the company's CEO's defense of the data only containing "minor inaccuracies...created using the best information available" seems to be a reference to SuperShadow. Spotts is also in reference to the popular Topps trading card manufacturers.

===[[Fanfiction]]===
''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]'' mentions how [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]] ''"entered the folkloric cycles of several primitive cultures, where she often was merged with local historical figures or goddess-characters... Even today, educated immigrants from those cultures will write fictive cycles about her, some of them amazingly prurient."''

===Fan letter===
[[Firmus Piett/Legends|Firmus Piett]] was not originally intended to appear in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']], and was not in the script. However, during the filming, a fan mail convinced [[George Lucas]] to include him.

===[[R2-KT/Legends|R2-KT]]===
The droid [[R2-KT/Legends|R2-KT]] was created by a member of the 501st Legion fan organization, and it eventually was given an appearance in ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series.

===[[Han shot first]]===
The "[[Han shot first]]" observation which became a motto of [[W:c:swfans:Movie purism|purist]] fans has been referenced as an [[Han shot first#Repercussions in Star Wars media|in-joke in official Star Wars media]]. For example, in the game ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'' it is possible, while playing as [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], to hear the enemy troops exclaim, "It's Solo, and he's shooting first- that's not fair!". In the novel ''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'', [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] remarks that Han should learn to shoot first. In ''[[Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy]]'' ([[Star Wars Insider 89]]), an in-universe report states that Han "is prone to 'shooting first'." There are several other references in the New Jedi Order time period and beyond where Leia makes remarks about Han always shooting first.

==Characters named after fans==
{{Main|Tuckerization}}
On occasion, authors name their characters after Star Wars fans that are well known in the community.
*[[Ausar Auset]] (named after a [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] forum member ausar_auset.<ref name="DHB">{{DHBoards|subdomain=www|url=boards/profile.php?mode=viewprofile&u=2550|thread=ausar_auset profile}}</ref>)
*[[Vob Bitas]] ([[Bob Vitas]])
*[[Durnar]] (named after a [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] forum member)
*[[JMM]] ([[James McFadden|James M. McFadden]])
*[[Lwothin]] (named after the online alias of [[Christopher McElroy]], Nightowl)
*[[Meeshal]] (named after [[Mish'al Samman]])
*[[Dama Montalvo]] (named after a [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] forum member)
*[[Nict]] (named after [[Jedi Council Forums]] member AdmiralNick22)
*[[Orrimaarko/Legends|Orrimaarko]] ([[Orrin Marko]])
*[[Pepin]] ([[Dany Pépin]])
*[[R2-KT/Legends|R2-KT]] ([[Katie Johnson]])
*[[Krono Relt]] ([[Nathan Butler]])
*[[Tap-Nar-Pal]] (named after a [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] forum member)
*[[T'Bolton]] (named after a [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] forum member)
*[[Dunc T'racen]] ([[Tracy Duncan]])
*[[Xeltek]] (named after the message board screen name of ''Star Wars'' fan Christian Florbrant)
*[[Ram Zerimar]] (named after [[Raymond Ramirez]], [[StarWars.com]] blogger and Iraq war veteran)

{{IncompleteList|oou=1}}

==Art based on fans==
*[[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] in ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'', appearance modeled on a costume by [[Thomas J. Spanos]]<ref name="TFN post">{{JCFCite|url=posts/30459895|board=Authors and Artists|thread=The Official Jan Duursema Discussion Thread|user=[[Jan Duursema|Jan_Duursema]]|date=2009-09-29|time=6:22 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160521115027/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-jan-duursema-discussion-thread.5915607/page-94}}</ref>.
*[[Guri]], based on [[Leah Mangue]]. (by [[Joe Corroney]], ''[[Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy]]'')
*[[Ganner Krieg]], based on [[Thomas J. Spanos]].<ref name="TFN post" />
*[[Krono Relt]], based on [[Nathan Butler]]. ([[Empire 13|''Empire'' 13]])
*[[Ishin-Il-Raz]], based on [[Maikel Das]], a regular contributor for the [http://www.oswfc.de Official German Star Wars Fan Club magazine]. (by Joe Corroney, ''[[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals]]'' and ''[[Republic HoloNet News Special Inaugural Edition 16:5:241]]'')
*[[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] (in a young clone body holding the [[Holocron of Bodo-Baas]]) in ''[[20 Most Memorable Moments of the Expanded Universe]]'' by Joe Corroney, appearance modeled on a costume by [[Thomas J. Spanos]]
*[[Shea Sadashassa/Legends|Shea Sadashassa]], based on [[Roseanna Chickos]], a Boston Rebels FanForce member. (by [[Joe Corroney]], ''Republic HoloNet News Special Inaugural Edition 16:5:24'')

==Mistaken identity==
When an author or artist un-intentionally implements a fan idea, due to mistakenly believing it to already be canon.

===[[Cygnus Spaceworks/Legends|Cygnus Spaceworks]]===
Apparently originating in ''[[Book of Imperial Shuttle Plans: Cygnus Spaceworks]]'', a series of fan-made blueprints for the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]], Cygnus Spaceworks has been mentioned in such references as ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' and ''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' as the manufacturer of ''Lambda''-class shuttles and similar vessels. A reference to the [[Unification Wars (Republic)|Unification Wars]] in ''[[Star Wars Revenge of the Sith: Incredible Cross-Sections]]'' may also be inspired by the same source.

===[[Imperator-class Star Destroyer|''Imperator''-class Star Destroyer]]===
In ''[[Star Wars Revenge of the Sith: Incredible Cross-Sections]]'', it was stated that the ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer was initially designated ''Imperator''-class, and this term has been repeated in subsequent sources with reference to the earliest years of the Empire. Like the name of Cygnus Spaceworks, this term derives from a set of old, and apparently fan-made blueprints, hull-plans for a "Star Destroyer - Imperator Class". As a matter of trivia, it is worth mentioning in this context that the [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel Star Wars]] comics did use a distinct designation for Star Destroyers; but this was "''Star Destroyer''-class Battle Cruiser".

===[[K'Kruhk]]'s lightsaber===
The design for K'Kruhk's lightsaber was developed by a fan and displayed on his website. Artist [[Jan Duursema]] implemented the design into ''[[Jedi: Mace Windu]]'', mistakenly thinking that the design was from a canon source.

===[[Sian Jeisel]]===
Jeisel's first name was the result of a misunderstanding. Jan Duursema's daughter, Sian, dressed as Jeisel for [[Celebration III]]. A photograph of her from that event was captioned "Sian Jeisel"; fans confused this as the character's full name, rather than the name of the person in the costume followed by what the costume portrayed. This confusion apparently led to ''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'' using this name in text, making it official.<ref name="Jeisel">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24223339|board=Literature|thread=When Jeisel get a first name?|user=Jan_Duursema|date=2006-06-25|time=5:10 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801162759/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/when-jeisel-get-a-first-name.24223226}}</ref>

===[[Trinto Duaba]] in ''[[Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens]]''===
Unsubstantiated fan theories placed [[Salo Gardner]] as the actor for [[Trinto Duaba]] in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] due to the actor's similar appearance to the onscreen alien (despite the fact that Duaba's appearance was achieved by a one-piece rubber mask created by [[Rick Baker]] and not makeup).<ref name="Trevas">{{WebCite|url=http://jedinews.co.uk/news/news.aspx?newsID=21739|text=UPDATE: The Force Awakens: Salo Gardener Returns|date=2015-05-05|work=[https://www.jedinews.com jedinews.com]|archivedate=20150511003435}}</ref> When Gardner later appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens]]'', news sites erroneously made the connection to Trinto Duaba based on the disproven myth.<ref name="Yahoo TFA">{{WebCite|url=https://uk.movies.yahoo.com/amazing-nod-to-original-star-wars-spotted-in-new-118283219266.html|text=Amazing Nod To Original Star Wars Spotted In New Force Awakens Photos|author=Butler, Tom|date=2015-05-06|work=[[Wikipedia:Yahoo!|Yahoo Movies UK]]|archivedate=20201108124740}}</ref> When Gardner's new character was featured in ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Force Awakens]]'', the same mistake was made and the character was identified as Trinto Duaba.<ref name="LEGO TFA">''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Force Awakens]]''</ref>

==Official invitations==
When [[Lucasfilm]] specifically invites fans to provide input into the Star Wars universe. While these elements were technically never "fanon", they were created by the fans and deserve mention here.

===[[The Fantastic Technology Contest]]===
For the book ''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'', [[West End Games]] and the [[Role Playing Game Association]] held a contest allowing players of [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]] to submit various examples of technology; winning entries were included in the book. Out of about seventy-five submissions, nearly half were included in the book, with first place going to [[Michael Zebrowski]] for his [[Repulsor Grappling Gun]].

===[[Design An Alien]] contest===
[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine|''Star Wars Galaxy'']] magazine held a contest inviting fans to submit artwork and descriptions of original Star Wars aliens. These winning alien designs were published in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 7]].

===Clone Wars Jedi poll===
For the ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'' animated series, [[Cartoon Network]] held an online poll which allowed viewers to pick which of three [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] would be featured in an upcoming episode. The winning choice, [[Voolvif Monn]], appeared in episodes [[Clone Wars Chapter 20|20]] and [[Clone Wars Chapter 22|22]]. The two other choices, [[Foul Moudama]] and [[Roron Corobb/Legends|Roron Corobb]], appeared later in the series.

===Episode III: You Pick the Droid Color===
[[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] members were invited to vote for the color scheme of [[R4-G9/Legends|R4-G9]] appearing in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']].

===[[What's The Story?]]===
A monthly invitation for members of [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] to provide a backstory for a certain element of the films or EU. While the main subject already exists in the universe, fans are able to introduce additional new elements in their entries. Winning entries are published in the [[Databank (original)|Databank]].

===[[Darth Who Contest]] -- Name the Newest Sith Lord!===
Members of [[StarWars.com]] were given the chance to give [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] his [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] name: Darth _____. Five fan-submitted names were nominated: '''Darth Acheron''', '''Darth Caedus''' (which was the winner), '''Darth Judicar''', '''Darth Paxis''' and '''Darth Taral'''. Voting concluded on February 27th, 2007, and the winning submission was included in the novel [[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]], from Del Rey publishing. [http://www.starwars.com/darthwho]

===''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]'' [[canon]]icity===
The [[LucasArts]] [http://www.lucasarts.com website] held a poll to determine how ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]'' would be fit into continuity. The options were:
#As much of it as possible should be included.
#The basic storyline, but without a lot of the gameplay elements, should be included.
#Only things like the units and characters should be considered part of continuity.
#None of it should be included.
#I really don't worry about such things.

==Notes and references==
{{Reflist}}

[[Category:Real-world lists|Fanon]]
[[Category:Star Wars culture]]
